title: School Appraisal v2 ↔ Principal Observation Rubric — Cross-Reference Mapping
description: Explicit mapping between school_appraisals_v2 (5 domains × 31 aspects) and principal-observation-rubric.json (8 foci P1-P8 × 55 evidence indicators). Both instruments are completed during the same Specialist + Director visit window (Sept-Oct, Feb-Apr). This mapping prevents double-counting, prevents gaps, and enables data triangulation across the two instruments.
context: When an Eduversal HQ Specialist visits a partner school for the school_appraisals_v2 cycle, an EPDP-equipped Director or Specialist also conducts a Principal Leadership Observation using principal-observation-rubric.json. Both cover overlapping ground (Leadership, Teaching Quality, Operations, etc.) but from different angles: school appraisal = institution-as-system; principal rubric = principal-as-leader. Without an explicit mapping, observers risk either redundant work or gaps where each instrument assumes the other covered something.
design_principles: Each school_appraisals_v2 aspect maps to ZERO, ONE, or MORE principal rubric foci.
Each principal rubric focus maps to ZERO, ONE, or MORE school_appraisals_v2 aspects.
Where mapping exists, the relationship is one of TRIANGULATION (corroboration) — both instruments produce evidence about the same underlying reality from different angles.
Where mapping is empty, the school appraisal aspect or principal rubric focus stands alone — no corroboration available, evidence is single-source.
Mapping is many-to-many; do NOT enforce one-to-one.

d1-a1.school_aspect_summary: School has clearly articulated vision and mission underpinned by core educational values
d1-a1.principal_focus_summary: P1 Strategic Vision & School Improvement Planning — 'visi-misi visibly displayed and accurately recalled by staff and students' is a P1 evidence_indicator
d1-a1.triangulation_note: School appraisal asks: does the school articulate this? Principal rubric asks: does the principal embody and operationalise this? Strong school + weak principal evidence = vision is institutional but not lived through leadership.

d1-a2.school_aspect_summary: Leadership teams established for all school development goals
d1-a2.triangulation_note: P1 captures whether teams exist and are aligned to plan; P5 captures whether the principal develops capacity within those teams.

d1-a3.school_aspect_summary: Effective organisational structure facilitating transparent communication
d1-a3.triangulation_note: P3 internal operations dimension; P6 stakeholder dimension.

d1-a4.school_aspect_summary: Structured performance management for all staff including KPIs for leaders
d1-a4.triangulation_note: P5 evidence_indicator: 'Teacher Appraisal System (TAS) implementation evidence — observation cycles, appraisal records'.

d1-a5.school_aspect_summary: School operates within legal and regulatory framework. Governance transparent and inclusive
d1-a5.triangulation_note: P7 compliance dimension; P8 personal-conduct dimension (transparency in decision-making).

d2-a1.school_aspect_summary: Teachers produce comprehensive lesson and unit plans aligned to standards
d2-a1.triangulation_note: P2 indicator: 'Curriculum maps and schemes of work are quality-assured by the principal' — school evidence is plan QUALITY; principal evidence is principal's QA discipline.

d2-a2.school_aspect_summary: Clear, well-documented assessment policies. Variety of assessment strategies
d2-a2.triangulation_note: P2 = pedagogical leadership; P4 = data-driven QA dimension.

d2-a3.school_aspect_summary: Students actively involved in understanding their own learning progress
d2-a3.triangulation_note: Single-source corroboration via P2 instructional leadership presence.

d2-a4.school_aspect_summary: Constructive and specific feedback provided to students on assignments and assessments

d2-a5.school_aspect_summary: Teaching systematically evaluated through PD records, peer reviews, observations
d2-a5.triangulation_note: P2 = pedagogical layer; P5 = staff supervision/development layer. The principal's appraisal/walkthrough discipline is captured here.

d2-a6.school_aspect_summary: School uses LMS actively with teachers, students, and parents
d2-a6.triangulation_note: Operational dimension — system in place and used.

d3-a1.school_aspect_summary: Each subject department well-organised with member list, qualified leader
d3-a1.triangulation_note: P5 evidence: 'Annual PD plan tied to appraisal findings and school priorities' — principal as architect of the structure school appraisal observes.

d3-a2.school_aspect_summary: Subject department meetings held biweekly, focus on academic improvement

d3-a4.school_aspect_summary: Teachers receive regular formal observations and frequent walkthroughs
d3-a4.triangulation_note: Walkthrough rhythm is the operational manifestation of P2 (instructional leadership) + P5 (staff supervision discipline).

d3-a5.school_aspect_summary: Active mentoring programme pairing experienced teachers with newer colleagues

d3-a7.school_aspect_summary: Curriculum incorporates diverse cultural perspectives and authentic learning

d3-a8.school_aspect_summary: Comprehensive digital policy governing technology use
d3-a8.triangulation_note: P7 captures PP_17_2025 digital child protection alignment — single school appraisal aspect maps to two principal foci.

d4-a1.school_aspect_summary: School tracks student achievement data systematically

d4-a3.triangulation_note: GAP — no principal rubric focus directly covers this. Single-source evidence; this aspect of school appraisal stands alone.

d4-a4.school_aspect_summary: School identifies and supports advanced students with differentiation

d4-a6.school_aspect_summary: Systematic processes for identifying students with additional learning needs

d5-a1.school_aspect_summary: Students demonstrate behaviours consistent with school's mission and values
d5-a1.triangulation_note: P1 = strategic alignment; P8 = principal modelling values that students reflect.

d5-a2.school_aspect_summary: Robust safeguarding and child protection systems

d5-a3.school_aspect_summary: Effective internal and external communication processes

d5-a4.school_aspect_summary: School promotes high-quality educational programmes and co-curricular activities

d5-a5.school_aspect_summary: School actively involves parents and wider community as partners

d5-a6.school_aspect_summary: School actively monitors and supports staff well-being

during_visit.single_visit_dual_instrument: Specialist + Director conduct school_appraisals_v2 visit alongside principal_observations entry. Single trip, two outputs.
during_visit.evidence_corroboration_rule: Where a school appraisal aspect and a principal rubric focus map to each other, observers cross-check: does the institutional evidence match the leadership evidence? A high school-aspect rating WITHOUT corresponding strong leadership evidence is a structural concern (the system works despite the leader, not because of them).
during_visit.divergence_protocol: If school aspect rating ≥ 3 AND mapped principal focus rating = D (Developing) — flag for QAR investigation. Conversely, if principal focus = E (Established) AND mapped school aspect rating ≤ 2 — flag for capacity-building (leader has skill, system lacks the supporting structures).

post_visit.report_structure: QAR Findings Report includes a Triangulation section with: aligned strengths (both instruments strong), aligned concerns (both instruments weak), divergences (mismatch — investigation required).

gaps_with_no_principal_rubric_coverage.comment: Single-source evidence. Principal rubric does not cover this specifically — no corroboration via leadership instrument. Phase-2 candidate for principal rubric expansion if a P9 or expanded P5 indicator is appropriate.

principal_foci_mapping_density._note: P2 (Instructional Leadership) and P5 (Staff Development) are the most cross-corroborated foci — 11 and 10 school aspects map to each. P8 (Personal Conduct) and P7 (Compliance) are least corroborated by the school appraisal — single-source for the leadership evidence in those areas.

implementation_notes: This JSON is read by Phase-2 reporting tools that produce the QAR triangulation section.
AH `school-appraisals.html` will surface 'related principal observation focus' chips on each aspect (Phase-2 UI work).
AH `ObservationEntry.html?type=principal_leadership` will surface 'related school appraisal aspects' chips on each focus (Phase-2 UI work).
principal_appraisal_v1.json F2 data_source remains principal_observations.fociResponses — school_appraisals_v2 ratings do NOT directly enter F2 to preserve instrument independence.

provenance.review_cadence: Annual (W37) — re-validate as either source instrument evolves
